title: overcoverage2
text: package provides reusable checks preparation helpers register data. functions help identify inconsistencies (example, emigration without re-immigration, activity death), standardize register variables creating model inputs.

title: overcoverage2
code: library(overcoverage2)  # data_long should be a person-year data.frame # with columns like id, year, firstimmig, death, emig, immig, reimmig, ... # # checks <- oc2_check_register_data( #   data_long, #   year_beginning = 2002, #   final_year = 2022 # ) # cleaned_data <- checks$data # removed_ids <- checks$removed

title: overcoverage2
text: helpers designed adapted countries different register definitions. can supply register lists, covariates, binary conversion rules keeping checks.
code: # register_cols <- c("married", "divorced", "amf", "studies", "intmove", #                    "child", "pension", "job", "social", "faminc_b") # # prepared <- oc2_prepare_register_data( #   cleaned_data, #   register_cols = register_cols, #   binary_rules = list( #     pension = list(source = "aldpens", threshold = 0), #     job = list(source = "forvers", threshold = 0), #     social = list(source = "socink", threshold = 0) #   ) # )

title: Authors and Citation
text: Lucy Brown. Author. Eleni Matechou. Author. Bruno Santos. Author, maintainer. Eleonora Mussino. Author.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Brown L, Matechou E, Santos B, Mussino E (2026). overcoverage2: Capture-Recapture Hidden Markov Models Register-Based Population Inference. R package version 0.1.0, https://brsantos.github.io/overcoverage2.
code: @Manual{,   title = {overcoverage2: Capture-Recapture Hidden Markov Models for Register-Based Population Inference},   author = {Lucy Brown and Eleni Matechou and Bruno Santos and Eleonora Mussino},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.1.0},   url = {https://brsantos.github.io/overcoverage2}, }

title: overcoverage2
text: Capture-Recapture Hidden Markov Models register-based population inference. package includes reusable checks detect inconsistencies register data modeling.

title: overcoverage2
text: Install GitHub: Linux, may need TBB RcppParallel installation, example:

title: overcoverage2
text: minimal example using simulated inputs. Replace data starting values.
code: library(overcoverage2)  set.seed(123) N <- 200 T <- 6 L <- 3  y <- matrix(sample(1:10, N * T, replace = TRUE), N, T) covariates <- matrix(rnorm(N * 11), N, 11) age <- array(rbinom(N * 2 * T, 1, .5), dim = c(N, 2, T)) tin <- array(rbinom(N * 2 * T, 1, .5), dim = c(N, 2, T)) combins <- matrix(sample(1:(64 * 2^(L - 2)), N * T, replace = TRUE), N, T) init_params <- rnorm(191)  fit <- model_BLB(   y = y,   covariates = covariates,   age = age,   tin = tin,   L = L,   combins = combins,   init_params = init_params,   num_bootstraps = 2,   threads = 1 )  fit

what: Compute mixing probabilities for a partition — compute_mixing_weights
title: Compute mixing probabilities for a partition — compute_mixing_weights
text: Computes mixing probabilities individual using compiled C++ routine mixing_probs. packaged wrapper function accepts inputs directly optionally saves results.

title: Compute mixing probabilities for a partition — compute_mixing_weights
code: compute_mixing_weights(   y,   covariates,   age,   tin,   combins,   estimates,   L = 8,   init = c(1, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0),   threads = 8,   cpp_file = NULL,   save_path = NULL )

title: Compute mixing probabilities for a partition — compute_mixing_weights
text: y Observation matrix (individuals x time). covariates Covariate matrix (individuals x 11). age Age indicator array (individuals x 2 x time). 3-level array supplied, third level used; 2-level array supplied, third level treated zeros. tin Time-indicator array (individuals x 2 x time). combins Register combination matrix. estimates Parameter estimates matrix (bootstraps x parameters). L Number registers. init Initial state distribution. threads Number threads RcppParallel. cpp_file Optional path C++ file source via Rcpp::sourceCpp. save_path Optional path save mixing weights RDS file.

title: Compute mixing probabilities for a partition — compute_mixing_weights
text: 3D array mixing weights (individuals x 2 x bootstraps).

what: Compute Viterbi paths for each individual — compute_viterbi_paths
title: Compute Viterbi paths for each individual — compute_viterbi_paths
text: Runs Viterbi algorithm using compiled C++ routine viterbi. Inputs provided directly; file reads happen outside wrapper.

title: Compute Viterbi paths for each individual — compute_viterbi_paths
code: compute_viterbi_paths(   y,   covariates,   age,   tin,   combins,   estimates,   mixing_weights,   L = 8,   init = c(1, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0),   threads = 8,   cpp_file = NULL,   save_path = NULL )

title: Compute Viterbi paths for each individual — compute_viterbi_paths
text: y Observation matrix (individuals x time). covariates Covariate matrix (individuals x 11). age Age indicator array (individuals x 2 x time). 3-level array supplied, third level used; 2-level array supplied, third level treated zeros. tin Time-indicator array (individuals x 2 x time). combins Register combination matrix. estimates Parameter estimates matrix (bootstraps x parameters). mixing_weights Mixing weights array (individuals x 2 x bootstraps). L Number registers. init Initial state distribution. threads Number threads RcppParallel. cpp_file Optional path C++ file source via Rcpp::sourceCpp. save_path Optional path save optimal paths RDS file.

title: Compute Viterbi paths for each individual — compute_viterbi_paths
text: 3D array optimal paths (individuals x states x bootstraps).

title: Run the BLB model — model_BLB
text: Runs BLB log-likelihood optimization loop model described paper. confidentiality, data reads commented ; inputs must provided directly.

title: Run the BLB model — model_BLB
code: model_BLB(   part = 20,   y = NULL,   covariates = NULL,   age = NULL,   tin = NULL,   combins = NULL,   init_params = NULL,   init = c(1, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0),   N = NULL,   L = 8,   num_bootstraps = 100,   boot_start = 1,   threads = 8,   progress_path = NULL,   results_path = NULL,   cpp_file = NULL )

title: Run the BLB model — model_BLB
text: part Partition index used file naming reading saving. y Observation matrix (individuals x time). covariates Covariate matrix (individuals x 11). age Age indicator array (individuals x 2 x time). 3-level array supplied, third level used; 2-level array supplied, third level treated zeros. tin Time-indicator array (individuals x 2 x time). combins Register combination matrix. init_params Initial parameter vector. init Initial state distribution. N Population size. Defaults nrow(y). L Number registers. num_bootstraps Number bootstrap runs. boot_start First bootstrap index run. threads Number threads RcppParallel. progress_path Optional path saving incremental results. results_path Optional path saving final estimates. cpp_file Optional path C++ file source via Rcpp::sourceCpp.

title: Run the BLB model — model_BLB
text: list elements estimates, means, sds,   results.

title: Run the BLB model — model_BLB
code: # See model_BLB_simulated_example() for a simulated run

title: Fit the non-BLB model — model_nonBLB
text: Fits model without Bag Little Bootstraps using compiled C++ log-likelihood routine loglikelihood_nonBLB_parallel. Inputs provided directly; file reads happen outside wrapper.

title: Fit the non-BLB model — model_nonBLB
code: model_nonBLB(   y,   covariates,   age,   tin,   combins,   L = 8,   init = c(1, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0),   sample_n = NULL,   seed = NULL,   init_params = NULL,   num_betas = 78,   num_deltas = 112,   threads = 8,   cpp_file = NULL,   save_path = NULL )

title: Fit the non-BLB model — model_nonBLB
text: y Observation matrix (individuals x time). covariates Covariate matrix (individuals x 11). age Age indicator array (individuals x 2 x time). 3-level array supplied, third level used; 2-level array supplied, third level treated zeros. tin Time-indicator array (individuals x 2 x time). combins Register combination matrix. L Number registers. init Initial state distribution. sample_n Optional sample size subsample individuals. seed Optional RNG seed used sampling generating init params. init_params Initial parameter vector. NULL, random vector used. num_betas Number beta parameters (default 78). num_deltas Number delta parameters (default 112). threads Number threads RcppParallel. cpp_file Optional path C++ file source via Rcpp::sourceCpp. save_path Optional path save parameter estimates RDS file.

title: Fit the non-BLB model — model_nonBLB
text: optimization result object nlm.

title: Check Register Data Consistency — oc2_check_register_data
text: Apply set consistency checks person-year register data return cleaned data alongside IDs removed check.

title: Check Register Data Consistency — oc2_check_register_data
code: oc2_check_register_data(   data,   id_col = "id",   year_col = "year",   firstimmig_col = "firstimmig",   death_col = "death",   emig_col = "emig",   immig_col = "immig",   reimmig_col = "reimmig",   year_beginning,   final_year )

title: Check Register Data Consistency — oc2_check_register_data
text: data data.frame long (person-year) format. id_col Column name individual identifiers. year_col Column name calendar year. firstimmig_col Column name first immigration year. death_col Column name death indicator (0/1). emig_col Column name emigration indicator (0/1). immig_col Column name immigration indicator (0/1). reimmig_col Column name re-immigration indicator (0/1). year_beginning First year study window. final_year Last year study window.

title: Check Register Data Consistency — oc2_check_register_data
text: list components: data Filtered data.frame removing inconsistent IDs. removed Named list ID vectors removed check.

what: Build Observation Inputs for model_BLB — oc2_prepare_model_BLB_inputs
title: Build Observation Inputs for model_BLB — oc2_prepare_model_BLB_inputs
text: Create observation matrices combinations BLB model prepared register data. helper intended internal use.

title: Build Observation Inputs for model_BLB — oc2_prepare_model_BLB_inputs
code: oc2_prepare_model_BLB_inputs(   data,   id_col = "id",   year_col = "year",   register_cols,   covariate_cols,   reimmig_col = "reimmig",   death_col = "death",   emig_col = "emig",   year_beginning,   final_year,   combins = NULL )

title: Build Observation Inputs for model_BLB — oc2_prepare_model_BLB_inputs
text: data data.frame long (person-year) format. id_col Column name individual identifiers. year_col Column name calendar year. register_cols Character vector register indicator columns. covariate_cols Character vector covariate columns include observation combinations. reimmig_col Column name re-immigration indicator (0/1). death_col Column name death indicator (0/1). emig_col Column name emigration indicator (0/1). year_beginning First year study window. final_year Last year study window. combins Optional matrix combination indices used unobserved years.

title: Build Observation Inputs for model_BLB — oc2_prepare_model_BLB_inputs
text: list elements `y_matrix`, `X`, `num_combos`.

what: Prepare Register Variables for Modeling — oc2_prepare_register_data
title: Prepare Register Variables for Modeling — oc2_prepare_register_data
text: Standardize clean register indicators (e.g., convert binary, replace missing values, zero-registers emigration/death).

title: Prepare Register Variables for Modeling — oc2_prepare_register_data
code: oc2_prepare_register_data(   data,   register_cols,   emig_col = "emig",   death_col = "death",   binary_rules = NULL,   na_to_zero = TRUE,   zero_on_emig = TRUE,   zero_on_death = TRUE )

title: Prepare Register Variables for Modeling — oc2_prepare_register_data
text: data data.frame long (person-year) format. register_cols Character vector register indicator columns. emig_col Column name emigration indicator (0/1). death_col Column name death indicator (0/1). binary_rules Optional named list describing binary conversions. entry include `source` optionally `threshold`. na_to_zero Logical; TRUE, replace NA 0 register columns. zero_on_emig Logical; TRUE, set register columns 0 emigration. zero_on_death Logical; TRUE, set register columns 0 death.

title: Prepare Register Variables for Modeling — oc2_prepare_register_data
text: data.frame cleaned register columns.
